item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
Courtney Kube
Phil Helsel
Freddie Clayton
Kyla Guilfoil
item.description: U.S. Central Command said it launched additional strikes against Iran on Saturday, hours after Iran said it had struck U.S.-linked targets in retaliation for American attacks the day before.

media:content.media:text: A drone view shows vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Musandam
media:content.media:description: Vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, as seen from Musandam, Oman, on June 15, 2026.

item.title: U.S. launches additional Iran strikes as tensions flare up over Hormuz

item.dc:creator: Elmira Aliieva
item.description: Fans from around the world have traveled thousands of miles to the World Cup, bringing with them displays of national identity that have delivered some of the tournament’s most iconic moments.

media:content.media:text: People wearing tartans walk down a street outside
media:content.media:description: Members of the Tartan Army make their way to South Station to board trains to Boston Stadium to watch the upcoming World Cup match between Scotland and Morocco in Boston on June 19, 2026.

media:content.media:text: World Cup fans bring vibrant celebrations to Times Square ahead of international matches
media:content.media:description: Fans of the Norwegian World Cup team perform the "Viking Row" in New York City on June 19.

media:content.media:text: Fans in Tartan walk on a ramp by the water
media:content.media:description: Scotland fans are pictured at the North East Tartan Army boat party ahead of Wednesday's FIFA World Cup match against Brazil, in Miami, on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: A woman poses next to a statue wearing a traffic cone for a photo her friend is taking
media:content.media:description: A Statue in James Michael Curley Park in Boston, Mass., wears a traffic cone as a woman poses for a photo on June 21, 2026. Orange traffic cones have been spotted on statues all over the city since the Tartan Army came to town.

media:content.media:text: Lumumba Vea stands on a pedestal in the stadium audience
media:content.media:description: Congolese superfan Michel Nkuka Mboladinga during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group K match between Colombia and Congo DR on Tuesday. He holds his stance for entire games in honor of the late Congo Prime Minister Patrice Lumumba.

item.title: Beyond the goals: The best World Cup trends charming U.S. soccer fans

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
item.description: The fairy-tale for Cape Verde continues as the tournament debutant proceeds to the knockout stage.

media:content.media:text: Cabo Verde players celebrate after the 0-0 draw during Cabo Verde v Saudi Arabia.
media:content.media:description: Cabo Verde players celebrate after the 0-0 draw during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group H match between Cabo Verde and Saudi Arabia at Houston Stadium on June 26, 2026 in Houston, Texas.

media:content.media:text: Firas al-Buraikan of Saudi Arabia in action during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group H match between Cape Verde and Saudi Arabia.
media:content.media:description: Firas al-Buraikan of Saudi Arabia in action during the match between Cape Verde and Saudi Arabia at Houston Stadium, on Friday.

media:content.media:text: Dailon Livramento #19 and Vozinha #1 of Cabo Verde celebrate after the 0-0 draw during Cabo Verde v Saudi Arabia.
media:content.media:description: Dailon Livramento #19 and Vozinha #1 of Cape Verde celebrate after the 0-0 draw.

item.title: World Cup debutant Cape Verde continues fairy-tale run to knockouts

item.description: Jude Bellingham se siente contento por el partido de Inglaterra, resaltando el trabajo hecho en la fase de grupos; además, reconoce la carrera de Harry Kane con los tres leones al convertirse en el máximo goleador de Inglaterra en la Copa Mundial.

media:content.media:title: Bellingham se rinde ante Kane: “Es increíble, es el máximo goleador de Inglaterra en el Mundial"
media:content.media:description: Jude Bellingham se siente contento por el partido de Inglaterra, resaltando el trabajo hecho en la fase de grupos; además, reconoce la carrera de Harry Kane con los tres leones al convertirse en el máximo goleador de Inglaterra en la Copa Mundial.

item.title: Bellingham se rinde ante Kane: “Es increíble, es el máximo goleador de Inglaterra en el Mundial"

item.description: El técnico de Inglaterra aseguró que el partido fue muy complicado, muy físico, pero lograron un triunfo importante ante Panamá, que los dejó como líderes de grupo y que ahora comienza su lucha por el título del Mundial.

media:content.media:title: Thomas Tuchel celebra la clasificación de Inglaterra a 16vos
media:content.media:description: El técnico de Inglaterra aseguró que el partido fue muy complicado, muy físico, pero lograron un triunfo importante ante Panamá, que los dejó como líderes de grupo y que ahora comienza su lucha por el título del Mundial.

item.title: Thomas Tuchel celebra la clasificación de Inglaterra a 16vos

item.description: El veterano Luka Modric ejecuta un centro preciso desde el banderín. Nikola se eleva en el aire, vence a los defensores ghaneses con un cabezazo con clase y manda el balón al fondo de la red.

media:content.media:title: Croacia toma delantera y se posiciona como segundo lugar del Grupo L
media:content.media:description: El veterano Luka Modric ejecuta un centro preciso desde el banderín. Nikola se eleva en el aire, vence a los defensores ghaneses con un cabezazo con clase y manda el balón al fondo de la red.

item.title: Croacia toma delantera y se posiciona como segundo lugar del Grupo L

item.description: Thomas Christiansen valoró la actitud y el compromiso de Panamá pese a la eliminación. El DT aseguró que el equipo compitió muy bien sus tres partidos, aunque reconoció que faltó puntería y mayor eficacia en ataque.

media:content.media:title: Christiansen: “Competimos muy bien los tres partidos”
media:content.media:description: Thomas Christiansen valoró la actitud y el compromiso de Panamá pese a la eliminación. El DT aseguró que el equipo compitió muy bien sus tres partidos, aunque reconoció que faltó puntería y mayor eficacia en ataque.

item.title: Christiansen: “Competimos muy bien los tres partidos”

item.description: José Córdoba lamentó la eliminación de Panamá tras la derrota ante Inglaterra, pero resaltó el esfuerzo del equipo frente a un rival de primer nivel. El defensor aseguró que esta generación tiene bases para seguir creciendo y competir mejor en el futuro

media:content.media:title: Córdoba: “Estoy orgulloso del sacrificio de este equipo”
media:content.media:description: José Córdoba lamentó la eliminación de Panamá tras la derrota ante Inglaterra, pero resaltó el esfuerzo del equipo frente a un rival de primer nivel. El defensor aseguró que esta generación tiene bases para seguir creciendo y competir mejor en el futuro

item.title: Córdoba: “Estoy orgulloso del sacrificio de este equipo”

item.description: Tras una revisión de VAR por un posible fuera de juego, los ghaneses empatan el partido después de una jugada a balón parado que termina en las redes de Croacia gracias a la zurda de Luckassen.

media:content.media:title: ¡Gol válido tras revisión arbitral! Derrick Luckassen mete zurda para empate
media:content.media:description: Tras una revisión de VAR por un posible fuera de juego, los ghaneses empatan el partido después de una jugada a balón parado que termina en las redes de Croacia gracias a la zurda de Luckassen.

item.title: ¡Gol válido tras revisión arbitral! Derrick Luckassen mete zurda para empate

item.description: Inglaterra está tomando la cima del grupo al derrotar a Panamá con una gran actuación de Bellingham y Kane. Distintas reacciones enmarcan el duelo en New Jersey.

media:content.media:title: ¡Así se vive el Mundial! Inglaterra y sus fans ya saborean el liderato del Grupo L
media:content.media:description: Inglaterra está tomando la cima del grupo al derrotar a Panamá con una gran actuación de Bellingham y Kane. Distintas reacciones enmarcan el duelo en New Jersey.

item.title: ¡Así se vive el Mundial! Inglaterra y sus fans ya saborean el liderato del Grupo L

item.description: El técnico aseguró que algunos de sus futbolistas han tenido mucho desgaste en lo que va del torneo y aprovechará este partido para rotar en algunas posiciones, sin importar que podrían perder el liderato del grupo si llegan a perder ante Portugal.

media:content.media:title: Néstor Lorenzo le dará descanso a jugadores clave de Colombia ante Portugal
media:content.media:description: El técnico aseguró que algunos de sus futbolistas han tenido mucho desgaste en lo que va del torneo y aprovechará este partido para rotar en algunas posiciones, sin importar que podrían perder el liderato del grupo si llegan a perder ante Portugal.

item.title: Néstor Lorenzo le dará descanso a jugadores clave de Colombia ante Portugal

item.description: Jude Bellingham levantó un preciso centro al área y Harry Kane ganó por lo alto con un certero cabezazo para ampliar la ventaja de Inglaterra frente a Panamá.

media:content.media:title: Cabezazo letal de Harry Kane para el segundo de Inglaterra
media:content.media:description: Jude Bellingham levantó un preciso centro al área y Harry Kane ganó por lo alto con un certero cabezazo para ampliar la ventaja de Inglaterra frente a Panamá.

item.title: Cabezazo letal de Harry Kane para el segundo de Inglaterra

item.description: El técnico del conjunto portugués hizo una modificación importante en la mediacancha, apostando por un hombre de mayor experiencia para un partido fundamental ante Colombia, donde buscarán quedarse con el liderato del grupo.

media:content.media:title: Roberto Martíenz explica el cambio en la alineación de Portugal contra Colombia
media:content.media:description: El técnico del conjunto portugués hizo una modificación importante en la mediacancha, apostando por un hombre de mayor experiencia para un partido fundamental ante Colombia, donde buscarán quedarse con el liderato del grupo.

item.title: Roberto Martíenz explica el cambio en la alineación de Portugal contra Colombia

item.dc:creator: Bridget Bowman
item.description: President Donald Trump’s endorsement will once again be on the ballot Saturday in the GOP primary runoff for Senate in Louisiana.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image of Julia Letlow, left, and John Fleming
media:content.media:description: U.S. Senate candidates Julia Letlow, left, and John Fleming.

item.title: Trump’s endorsement faces another test in Louisiana Senate primary runoff

item.dc:creator: Aria Bendix
item.description: At least three people have died in flash floods caused by heavy rain and multiple thunderstorms in Kentucky, officials said, with additional fatalities expected to be confirmed soon.

media:content.media:text: A line of vehicles driving through floodwater.
media:content.media:description: Flooding after heavy rainfall in Owensboro, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Andy Beshear speaking in front of an array of microphones.
media:content.media:description: Flooding after heavy rainfall in Owensboro, Ky. on Saturday.

item.title: Kentucky flash floods kill at least 3 people with rescue operations ongoing

item.dc:creator: Zoe Malin
item.description: Amazon Prime Day 2026 may be over, but some of the deals aren’t. Here are the best sales still live, including Medicube, Oura Ring, Apple and more editor favorites.

media:content.media:text: Amazon Prime Day officially ended on June 26, but some brands are extending their deals through the weekend for last-minute shoppers.
media:content.media:description: Amazon Prime Day officially ended on June 26, but some brands are extending their deals through the weekend for last-minute shoppers.

media:content.media:text: Amazon Prime Day officially ended on June 26, but some brands are extending their deals through the weekend for last-minute shoppers.
media:content.media:description: Amazon Prime Day officially ended on June 26, but some brands are extending their deals through the weekend for last-minute shoppers.

item.title: Amazon Prime Day is over, but I found 131+ deals still available — shop before you miss them

item.description: Fresh off the NBA Finals, teams are preparing for the 2026 draft where fresh talent will get to shine. NBC News' Marquis Francis reports on what to expect and how the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade is shaking up the league.

media:content.media:title: Game on: NBA prepares for 2026 draft
media:content.media:description: Fresh off the NBA Finals, teams are preparing for the 2026 draft where fresh talent will get to shine. NBC News' Marquis Francis reports on what to expect and how the Giannis Antetokounmpo trade is shaking up the league.

item.title: Game on: NBA prepares for 2026 draft

item.dc:creator: Aria Bendix
item.description: At least three people have died in flash floods caused by heavy rain and multiple thunderstorms in Kentucky, officials said, with additional fatalities expected to be confirmed soon.

media:content.media:text: A line of vehicles driving through floodwater.
media:content.media:description: Flooding after heavy rainfall in Owensboro, Ky. on Saturday.

media:content.media:text: Andy Beshear speaking in front of an array of microphones.
media:content.media:description: Flooding after heavy rainfall in Owensboro, Ky. on Saturday.

item.title: Kentucky flash floods kill at least 3 people with rescue operations ongoing

item.dc:creator: Kyla Guilfoil
Julia Ainsley
Julie Tsirkin
Laura Strickler
item.description: President Donald Trump announced Saturday that he nominated Lance Schroyer, a former Oklahoma state trooper, to take over as director of Immigration and Customs Enforcement.“I am very pleased to announce that I have nominated Lance Schroyer to be our next ICE Director,” Trump wrote in a Truth Social post.

media:content.media:text: An ICE patch and badge on an officer in Minneapolis.
media:content.media:description: Schroyer currently serves as the senior advisor to the Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin,

item.title: Trump says he is nominating former Oklahoma state trooper Lance Schroyer to be ICE director

item.dc:creator: Aria Bendix
item.description: Dangerous fire conditions in the Southwest are threatening to worsen blazes in Utah, Nevada and New Mexico, putting many communities on high alert this weekend

media:content.media:text: A firefighting helicopter flies over the mountain landscape near Piute Reservoir.
media:content.media:description: A firefighting helicopter flies over the mountain landscape near Piute Reservoir while responding to the fast-moving Cottonwood Fire on June 25.

item.title: Wildfires rage across the Southwest as officials warn of ‘extremely critical’ conditions

item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
Marlene Lenthang
item.description: The increasingly desperate search for survivors i n Venezuela entered a third day Saturday as people dug through the rubble of collapsed homes and apartment buildings after the devastating one-two punch of 7.2 and 7.5 magnitude earthquakes, knowing time is running out.

media:content.media:text: Rescue workers.
media:content.media:description: Rescue workers search through the rubble three days after earthquakes struck La Guaira, Venezuela, on June 27.

item.title: Venezuela earthquake death toll rises above 1,400

item.dc:creator: Elmira Aliieva
item.description: Fans from around the world have traveled thousands of miles to the World Cup, bringing with them displays of national identity that have delivered some of the tournament’s most iconic moments.

media:content.media:text: People wearing tartans walk down a street outside
media:content.media:description: Members of the Tartan Army make their way to South Station to board trains to Boston Stadium to watch the upcoming World Cup match between Scotland and Morocco in Boston on June 19, 2026.

media:content.media:text: World Cup fans bring vibrant celebrations to Times Square ahead of international matches
media:content.media:description: Fans of the Norwegian World Cup team perform the "Viking Row" in New York City on June 19.

media:content.media:text: Fans in Tartan walk on a ramp by the water
media:content.media:description: Scotland fans are pictured at the North East Tartan Army boat party ahead of Wednesday's FIFA World Cup match against Brazil, in Miami, on Tuesday.

media:content.media:text: A woman poses next to a statue wearing a traffic cone for a photo her friend is taking
media:content.media:description: A Statue in James Michael Curley Park in Boston, Mass., wears a traffic cone as a woman poses for a photo on June 21, 2026. Orange traffic cones have been spotted on statues all over the city since the Tartan Army came to town.

media:content.media:text: Lumumba Vea stands on a pedestal in the stadium audience
media:content.media:description: Congolese superfan Michel Nkuka Mboladinga during the FIFA World Cup 2026 Group K match between Colombia and Congo DR on Tuesday. He holds his stance for entire games in honor of the late Congo Prime Minister Patrice Lumumba.

item.title: Beyond the goals: The best World Cup trends charming U.S. soccer fans

item.dc:creator: Yuliya Talmazan
item.description: Poland says it’s taking a stand over a bloody massacre.

media:content.media:text: Polish archaeologists work at the exhumation site of the World War II Volhynia massacre
media:content.media:description: Polish archaeologists work in April last year at the exhumation site of mass killings in the village of Puzniki, Ukraine.

media:content.media:text: Lviv Marks Day Of Defenders And Defendresses Of Ukraine
media:content.media:description: Polish archaeologists work in April last year at the exhumation site of mass killings in the village of Puzniki, Ukraine.

media:content.media:text: President-Elect Karol Nawrocki Inauguration
media:content.media:description: Polish archaeologists work in April last year at the exhumation site of mass killings in the village of Puzniki, Ukraine.

item.title: A historical feud threatens a key wartime alliance for Ukraine

item.dc:creator: Jeremy Mikula
Ashley Mowreader
item.description: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer resigns, there’s a new World Cup king, and Europe experiences record heat.
item.title: Earthquakes hit Latin America, and George Washington goes to London: The news quiz

item.dc:creator: Bridget Bowman
item.description: President Donald Trump’s endorsement will once again be on the ballot Saturday in the GOP primary runoff for Senate in Louisiana.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image of Julia Letlow, left, and John Fleming
media:content.media:description: U.S. Senate candidates Julia Letlow, left, and John Fleming.

item.title: Trump’s endorsement faces another test in Louisiana Senate primary runoff
